title: viola canina subsp. lactea
intro: Viola canina subsp. lactea, also known as White Dog Violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Violet family, characterized by its pale yellow to white flowers with a distinctive yellow center. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Viola canina subsp. lactea include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral activity. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are commonly used to treat various health issues, including skin irritations and respiratory problems.
